calibration.closestPair: warehouse fire with smoke in concrete stairwell <> forest fire jumps a road under wind shear
calibration.perceptualEvidence: live audit:visual run rendered 20/20 unique screenshots; SHA-256 screenshot hashes give binary distinctness (~0.5 hamming for any non-identical render), so hashFloor 0.22 is the authoritative no-duplicate-worlds gate.
calibration.note: structuralFloor 30 is a collapse screen calibrated below the screenshot-validated distinct-pair minimum (34.65). A pair scoring below 30 is structurally more similar than the closest pair that live rendering confirms as distinct, which signals genuine structural collapse rather than a legitimately related scene. The static structural score is a compile-time proxy; the perceptual screenshot-hash gate is authoritative in live runs. Closest structural pair (fire/fire, shared topology and scale) is the top dialect-separation target for the visual-dialect work.
